chunk_summary: Defines stunting, wasting, oedema, and underweight in undernutrition screening and classification, emphasizing anthropometric measures, clinical syndromes, and operational indices for risk stratification and care planning.
learning_objectives: Understand the definitions and diagnostic criteria for stunting, wasting, oedema, and underweight in undernutrition.
Learn the anthropometric indices used for screening acute and chronic malnutrition in children under 5 years and older age groups.
Recognize the importance of MUAC and BMI-for-age z-scores in triaging malnutrition risk.
Differentiate between acute and chronic malnutrition for appropriate care planning.
key_facts: Wasting is defined as WHZ or WLZ < -2 SD, with severe wasting < -3 SD.
Stunting is defined as HAZ < -2 SD, with severe stunting < -3 SD.
Nutritional oedema is bilateral pitting oedema starting in the feet and is treated as a severe acute malnutrition criterion.
Underweight is defined as WAZ < -2 SD but should not be used alone for acute risk triage.
MUAC <115 mm is a validated cut-off for acute malnutrition in children aged 6–59 months.
BMI-for-age z-scores (BAZ) are used for assessing acute undernutrition in older children and adolescents.
reference_quote: Wasting is defined as WHZ or WLZ < -2 SD, with severe wasting < -3 SD. Stunting is defined as HAZ < -2 SD, with severe stunting < -3 SD. Nutritional oedema is bilateral pitting oedema starting in the feet and upgrades severity regardless of weight appearance.
document_key_topics: Definitions of stunting, wasting, oedema, and underweight
Operational guidelines for triaging acute malnutrition risk
Etiologic and longitudinal care planning for malnutrition
